description:
Operating Area: East Mediterranean, Croatia

Charter Brand New Sunreef 60 in Croatia. Step aboard the new Sunreef 60 AHALYA, a beautifully crafted 4-cabin boat designed for comfort and adventure on the water. This vessel seamlessly blends elegance with functionality, making it the perfect escape for families or groups of friends. This model boasts a combination of exterior and interior areas easily flowing into one another.

blueprint.name: AHALYA

toys.label: Water skies adult and for children
Technogym bench with the exercise app

tenders: Rib ZAR 49 SPORT LUXURY 70 hp outboard

crew.name: Bozo Pavlovic
crew.bio:
NATIONALITY / AGE / LANGUAGE
Croatian / Born 1982 / ENGLISH, CROATIAN

QUALIFICATIONS
MCA/RYA Off shore Yacht Master 500GT, D2 Basic Safety
(SWTCW) Certificate, D11 gmdss, stcw IV/2

With the degree from the University of Physical education and sport, majoring in SAILING, Bozo can teach You everything about the skill and art of sailing.  During his student time, Bozo supported himself working as a skipper on different yachts up to 50bt, gaining experience that
will lead him to a permanent captain position on the new SUNREEF YACHT MODEL SUREEF 50  SOLITAIRE. Last few winters  Bozo spent employed as the Physical Education teacher in elementary school in his hometown of  Ploce, Croatia. Captain  Bozo absolutely loves his job and takes pride in his work, He is extremely hard-working, displaying both a committed and flexible approach with strong people
skills. He has an outgoing personality, is good with children, and always a great disposition. His interests include sport, traveling, and underwater photography

FAVOURITE PLACE ON THE ADRIATIC COAST
Island of Vis. Island beautiful in nature & history, and is far from the bustle of the mainland. Place to relax and unwind

crew.name: Marko Matei
crew.bio:
NATIONALITY / AGE / LANGUAGE
Croatian / Born 1980 / ENGLISH, CROATIAN

QUALIFICATIONS/SKILLS
Trained Chef, Culinary Arts Degree, Kitchen Management, D2 Basic Safety (STCW)

Chef Marko MateI was born in Zagreb in 1980. Although with no formal education,driven by his passion Marko Matei manage to form himself into a personal chef with an admirable experience. He is a highly driven and well-versed chef with a passion for creating innovative dishes focusing on the local organically produced ingredients. Matei is out of the box thinker, delivering the exception culinary service efficiently, but still no compromise the authenticity of the sailing area. Due to his experience working as the personal and private
chef, chef Matei gained profound knowledge of plating simple food and „fine dining“ in interesting and attractive manner. Having chef Matei onboard means enjoying a curated selection of gourmet meals multiple times a day. From menu customization to preparing and serving exquisite dishes, Matei strives for culinary excellence and ensures that each client onboard AHALYA receives a truly unique dining experience.

FAVOURITE PLACE ON THE ADRIATIC COAST
I’m always happy and excited to sail to the remote islands National Park Kornati, where a few local farmers and fisherman keep the traditon alive; the tradition of fishing technics, sheep husbandry, production of wine, olive oil and honey.

crew.name: Marina Andrijasevic
crew.bio:
NATIONALITY / AGE / LANGUAGE
Croatian / Born 1982 / ENGLISH, ITALIAN, CROATIAN

QUALIFICATIONS
Trained Stewardess, ENG1 Medical Certificate D2, Basic Safety (STCW) Certificate, Boat leader category B

A Marina Andrijasevic was born in Zagreb, Croatia. From her early age she was drawn to the art of food preparation and didn’t take long before she was pulled in and is still mesmerized by it. Being a visual person, Marina makes everything prettier, weather it is the meals she prepares, drinks she serves or making the yacht clean and tidy. Fluent in English Marina is happy to share with
our guests her knowledge about Croatian history, art & design, whether traditional or contemporary. Her interests include product design, gardening,photography and most of all - cooking and exploring local cuisines. Marina joined our team in 2016 as the stewardess/chef aboard s/y Lagoon 560 Arctic Queen, and quickly adjusted to the strict regime of living and working on the sea. She is ard working and has eye for details. Later she joind the crew onboard sy Lagoon 620 Opal, where she worked for two summer seasons. Marina is fluent in English & Italian. She is a bachelor of visual communication & graphic design, graduated at Academia Italiana/Firenze. Marina’s mission is making your holiday onboard AHALYA the best you ever had.

FAVOURITE PLACE ON THE ADRIATIC COAST
Her favorite spot in the Adriatic is island of Vis where some day, she hopes to grow old.
